diff_policy.fallback_rule: Use the feature-scoped diff only for closeout, cross-wave reconciliation, or when a wave manifest cannot be bounded safely.

wave_manifest_requirements.review_context_rule: State what changed in this wave, which earlier-wave contracts are intentionally assumed present, and what remains out of scope.
wave_manifest_requirements.prerequisite_rule: List earlier-wave files that this wave depends on for compilation or runtime behavior even if they are unchanged in the current diff.
wave_manifest_requirements.verification_rule: List the exact verification commands run for this wave so review reconciliation can distinguish test gaps from review noise.
wave_manifest_requirements.artifact_generation_rule: Wave diff artifacts must include both tracked changes and any untracked files in the manifest, using a no-index patch for untracked files when needed.

review_reconciliation_policy.scope_context_rejection_rule: If Gemini reports a blocker caused by an intentionally omitted prerequisite file that is already landed and recorded in an earlier accepted wave, reject that finding explicitly and tighten the next wave manifest context.
review_reconciliation_policy.accessibility_rule: New modals, popovers, and status surfaces should expose accessible roles or labels so tests can scope assertions to the intended surface.
review_reconciliation_policy.warning_triage_rule: Non-failing test warnings should be recorded as residual cleanup only when the behavior is otherwise verified and the warning does not hide a correctness issue.
